findings.issue: Banned filler verb 'unlocks' used in card constraint line: 'screening must be cleared before Learning unlocks'.
findings.fix: Replace with: 'screening must be cleared before the Learning Journey opens' (or 'before Learning becomes available'). Reference: tone-of-voice §7 — 'unlocks' → 'delivers / produces / changes' or rewrite around the actual verb.

findings.issue: Home card (index.html:606) and catalog row (catalog/index.html:276) both say 'Live · 29 - Apr - 2026'. The /samarth page now reads 'Live · limited pilot · Apr 2026'. Sources confirm the prod cutover happened in Apr 2026 with a limited pilot — the specific 29-Apr date isn't cited in either compilation artefact. Either: (a) update home + catalog to 'Live · limited pilot · Apr 2026' to match the page, or (b) restore '29 - Apr - 2026' on the page if a verifiable source for that exact date exists outside this compilation folder.
findings.fix: Recommended: align home + catalog with the page's 'Live · limited pilot · Apr 2026' framing — matches the Module Work Breakdown's Phase 6 language and avoids overclaim. If the 29-Apr date is verifiable from a source not in the compilation folder, restore it on the page.

findings.issue: 'Naveent (RIL)' — likely a typo for 'Naveen'. Carried over from the prior version of the page; not in either compilation artefact (HRBP deck or Module Work Breakdown). Names matter at Apex level (tone-of-voice §1 rule 5) — wrong spelling reads as carelessness.
findings.fix: Confirm spelling with Nishant Amin or Ashish Agrawal. Likely 'Naveen' — fix here and re-verify against any prior comm where the name appeared.

findings.issue: organisation/index.html:146 lists Samarth team headcount as '~15' with PMs Sai/Manasa/Karan and Nishant Amin as lead. The /samarth squad section now adds Vikas Singh + Yash on engineering. Not a contradiction (those two were UI/SSO collaborators, not core team), but if the org page is meant to reflect the full extended squad, the headcount may now be ~17.
findings.fix: Decision call. If org page tracks core-team-only, leave at ~15 and ensure Vikas/Yash are listed under their actual home teams. Otherwise update.

findings.issue: Phrase 'Behaviour parity, not schema parity' is verbatim from the source doc but is jargon-heavy for an Apex reader. The next sentence in the doc explains it ('Supabase auto-handles RLS, signed URLs, OTP, session — each had to be re-thought, not mechanically copied'); the page omits the explainer, leaving the bare phrase to land cold.
findings.fix: Either drop 'Behaviour parity, not schema parity' or expand inline: 'rebuilt for behaviour parity (not just schema parity) — Supabase auto-handles RLS, signed URLs, OTP, sessions; each had to be re-thought from scratch on Node.'

findings.issue: All six candidate-journey steps carry the 'Live' pill. Source supports this (HRBP deck says all six steps are in the prod build, and Phase 6 confirms cutover). However, Step 06 'Progress' is described as 'Associate brings the certificate to the Store Manager' — i.e., the step happens *outside* the platform today (LMS+IJP integration is Q4 roadmap). The 'Live' pill on Step 06 is technically defensible (the step exists in the journey) but skirts overclaim.
findings.fix: Optional. Either keep 'Live' (the step's *outcome* — handoff to Store Manager — does happen today) or downgrade Step 06 to 'Live · external handoff' to make the platform-vs-process boundary explicit.

strengths: Hero stat strip is exec-readable: Stage / Role transitions / Form factor / Formats covered — no engineering-jargon tiles, every number sourceable.
§04 7-month delivery table is the page's strongest move — six phases, named environments (Dev → SIT → UAT → Prod), one-line headlines that survive an Apex skim while the source doc is one click away.
Honesty register is well-handled: 'Live · limited pilot' in the hero pill is the correct rendering of Phase 6's 'Prod (limited pilot)' source language; doesn't overclaim a full rollout.

Sources block earns its place per the tone-of-voice §3 carve-out — two distinct external artefacts (HRBP deck + engineering breakdown), each linked to its compilation-folder path with verb 'Open the deck →' / 'Open the breakdown →'.
Section-label run is contiguous (01 → 08, no gaps, no renumber drift) — passes the §10 common-issues check after the Sub-IPs grid was cut.
